title: French cinema faces funding crisis: 'There will inevitably be fewer films in the future'
subhead: The entire French film industry is under strain, affecting producers, distributors and cinema operators alike. This is likely to lead to a drop in the number of feature films produced in the coming years.

Russia increasingly vulnerable to deep-strike attacks by Ukrainian drones
subhead: Territories up to 2,000 kilometers from the Ukrainian border, previously considered untouchable due to their remoteness, are now being struck on a regular basis.

title: G7 finance ministers meet in Paris to overcome their differences
subhead: The G7 Finance Summit convenes in France on Monday and Tuesday, as US President Donald Trump has shattered the founding principles of multilateralism and free trade that have defined this club of wealthy nations.

title: New book by Canadian garbage collector-anthropologist becomes social phenomenon
subhead: The author and garbage collector, who juggled waste collection work with higher education studies, has written a book about his experience.

title: Over 50 Michelin-starred chefs demand change: 'Competitiveness only delivers soulless volume, sustainable agriculture creates real wealth'
subhead: 51 Michelin-starred chefs urge an urgent shift toward a less intensive, less harmful agricultural model that prioritizes sustainable, family-run and high-quality production.

title: Captured, imprisoned and tortured: Russia 'ignores all laws and does whatever it wants' to Ukrainian civilians
subhead: Since the 2022 invasion, Moscow has been arresting citizens from the occupied territories en masse in an effort to crush all resistance. More than 16,000 people are currently imprisoned, according to the Ukrainian Parliament Commissioner for Human Rights.

title: Elon Musk attacks Christopher Nolan for casting Lupita Nyong'o as Helen of Troy
subhead: The billionaire accused the filmmaker of 'defiling Homer' by choosing a Black actress, claiming Nolan only did so to comply with diversity requirements and win an Oscar. The controversy echoes the Trump administration's opposition to affirmative action policies.
